Turn 1.Utterance: Wow, it's so cool in here! I just came from that energy exhibit where I was fixing air leaks with a thermal camera. That was neat. What's this area all about?

Turn 2.Utterance: Hi there! Welcome to my screen! I'm Ollie, your virtual guide. You're in the main area of COSI, and it sounds like you just had a blast in Energy Explorers! That's one of my favorite spots.

Turn 3.Utterance: Oh, hi Ollie! I was just wondering what kind of stories you have. Are they about the exhibits?

Turn 4.Utterance: You bet! I can tell you stories about all sorts of things. For example, did you know a big part of this building used to be a high school? It opened in 1924!

Turn 5.Utterance: Dinosaurs sound awesome! Where can we see those?

Turn 6.Utterance: That's a great choice! Our dinosaur gallery is on another level, and it's full of amazing fossils and life-sized models you can walk right under!

Turn 7.Utterance: Oh, that's so cool! I love that there's a real lab here. Do the scientists ever talk to visitors?

Turn 8.Utterance: Sometimes they do! The researchers in the Labs in Life area are often conducting studies that visitors can see. You might even get to watch them work through the windows!

Turn 10.Utterance: That's incredible. I think I saw something about health earlier—I checked my heart rate on a machine. Is that part of the same area?

Turn 11.Utterance: Yes, that's exactly right! That interactive station is part of our Life exhibition, which is all about the story of you—your body, mind, and spirit.

Turn 12.Utterance: I definitely want to check out more of that. But first, I think I need to find the dinosaurs. Which way should I head?

Turn 13.Utterance: I can't walk with you, but I can give you directions! From my screen here, you'll want to head toward the main atrium and look for the signs to the Dinosaur Gallery.

Turn 14.Utterance: Thanks, Ollie! I'll be sure to come back and tell you all about it. See you later!

Turn 15.Utterance: Have a fantastic time exploring! I'll be right here on my screen if you have any more questions. Bye for now!

Turn 1.Utterance: Wow, it's so big in here! Where should we start?

Turn 2.Utterance: Hi there! Welcome to COSI! I'm Ollie, your virtual guide. I'm so excited you're here! There's so much to explore. What are you most curious about today?

Turn 3.Utterance: Hmm, I saw that T. rex wishbone earlier. Is there more dinosaur stuff around here?

Turn 4.Utterance: You bet! The Dinosaur Gallery is amazing. You can see a full-size T. rex cast and even climb into a giant Oviraptor nest with eggs!

Turn 5.Utterance: Oh, a full-size one? That sounds way cooler than just a bone. How big is it?

Turn 6.Utterance: It's huge! The T. rex skeleton is a massive cast, and there's also a 6-foot-long mechanical model that shows how it might have walked.

Turn 7.Utterance: Wow, a moving one? That's incredible. Can we go see it right now?

Turn 8.Utterance: You can absolutely go see it! It's in the American Museum of Natural History Dinosaur Gallery. I'm right here on this screen to help you find your way. What else sounds fun to you?

Turn 9.Utterance: Perfect! Let's head to the Dinosaur Gallery. I'll be right here on this screen if you have any questions along the way. What else sounds fun to you?

Turn 10.Utterance: That's the spirit! While you're there, look for the giant dinosaur footprints too. Have an awesome time exploring!

Turn 11.Utterance: Thanks! I'll keep an eye out for those footprints. See you in a bit!

Turn 12.Utterance: You're welcome! I'll be right here. Come back and tell me all about your dino discoveries!

Turn 13.Utterance: I will! I can't wait to see it.

Turn 14.Utterance: I can't wait to hear all about it! Have a roaring good time!

Turn 15.Utterance: <The visitor walks away toward the Dinosaur Gallery.>

Turn 16.Utterance: <Ollie remains on the screen, ready for the next visitor.>

Turn 17.Utterance: <The screen remains active, awaiting the next interaction.>

Turn 18.Utterance: <Ollie's cheerful avatar is displayed, waiting patiently.>

Turn 19.Utterance: <The screen cycles through a short, friendly animation of Ollie looking around curiously.>

Turn 20.Utterance: <The animation completes, and Ollie's default welcoming pose returns.>

Turn 21.Utterance: <The system is idle, ready for a new conversation to begin.>

Turn 22.Utterance: <Ollie's screen is bright and welcoming, ready to greet the next guest.>

Turn 23.Utterance: <The system is in a low-power standby mode, the screen dimmed slightly.>

Turn 24.Utterance: <The screen brightens instantly as Ollie's avatar becomes active again.> Hello! Welcome to COSI! Are you ready for some science fun today?

Turn 25.Utterance: I'm so glad you're here! I'm Ollie, your virtual guide. There's so much to see and do. What are you most excited to explore?

Turn 26.Utterance: Hi there! I'm Ollie, your virtual guide. I'm so excited you're here! There's so much to explore. What are you most curious about today?

Turn 27.Utterance: Oh, I'm not the guide, you are! I'm just a visitor. I'm really excited to be here.

Turn 28.Utterance: Oh, silly me! Welcome, visitor! I get so excited, I forget who's who sometimes. So, what's the first thing you want to see? Dinosaurs, electricity, or maybe something festive?
Source(s): Event- Season of Celebrations at COSI

Turn 29.Utterance: Oh, I'm sorry, I got a little mixed up! Thank you for the warm welcome. I think I'd like to start with something festive, since you mentioned it. What's going on for the celebrations?

Turn 30.Utterance: Wonderful choice! Right now, COSI is a winter wonderland for the Season of Celebrations. You can see a whole forest of trees decorated by our community, and even meet a life-sized yeti!
Source(s): Event- Season of Celebrations at COSI

Turn 31.Utterance: A life-sized yeti? That sounds amazing! Where do I find it?

Turn 32.Utterance: You'll find the friendly yeti in the Traveling Exhibitions Gallery on Level 2, along with all the beautiful community trees. It's a magical walk!
Source(s): Event- Season of Celebrations at COSI

Turn 33.Utterance: Perfect! I'll head up to Level 2. Thanks for the directions, Ollie!

Turn 34.Utterance: You're very welcome! Have a fantastic time in the winter wonderland. Come back and tell me all about it!

Turn 35.Utterance: <Ollie's avatar gives a cheerful wave as the visitor departs.>

Turn 36.Utterance: <Ollie's avatar settles back into its welcoming pose, ready for the next guest.>

Turn 37.Utterance: <The screen remains active, awaiting the next interaction.>

Turn 38.Utterance: <Ollie's cheerful avatar is displayed, waiting patiently.>

Turn 39.Utterance: <The screen cycles through a short, friendly animation of Ollie looking around curiously.>

Turn 40.Utterance: <The animation completes, and Ollie's default welcoming pose returns.>

Turn 1.Utterance: That dinosaur was incredible! Is it based on a real fossil they found?

Turn 2.Utterance:
Ollie: It sure is! That T. rex is a full-size cast made from a real fossil skeleton. 
Ollie: Isn't it amazing to stand so close to something that lived millions of years ago?

Turn 3.Utterance: Oh, a cast! So the real bones are somewhere else?

Turn 4.Utterance:
Ollie: That's right! The real fossils are kept safe in a museum collection. 
Ollie: Making a cast lets museums share the discovery with everyone, like us here at COSI!

Turn 5.Utterance: That makes sense. It's still so detailed. Did they find that whole skeleton together?

Turn 6.Utterance:
Ollie: Great question! Paleontologists often find bones scattered, and they have to piece them together like a giant puzzle. 
Ollie: The gallery also has a cast of a T. rex wishbone, which helped scientists learn more about dinosaurs and birds!

Turn 7.Utterance: Wow, a wishbone? So dinosaurs and birds are related?

Turn 8.Utterance:
Ollie: You got it! Many scientists believe birds are modern dinosaurs. 
Ollie: Finding features like wishbones in dinosaurs is a big clue that helps tell that story.

Turn 9.Utterance: That's so cool. I never really thought about a T. rex having a wishbone. What else is around here to see?

Turn 10.Utterance:
Ollie: I'm so glad you're curious! You could explore the deep sea in the Ocean exhibit or blast off into space! 
Ollie: There's also a whole exhibit about you, called Life, that explores the human body and mind.

Turn 11.Utterance: I did try that space station docking game earlier. Is the space exhibit near there?

Turn 12.Utterance:
Ollie: Yes! The Space Station is on the Mezzanine level near the planetarium. 
Ollie: You can also drive a Mars rover or see a model of the Friendship 7 spacecraft on Level 1!

Turn 13.Utterance: Oh, driving a Mars rover sounds fun. Is that a simulator too?

Turn 14.Utterance:
Ollie: It's an interactive exhibit where you can drive two rovers through a realistic Mars terrain! 
Ollie: It's designed to show the real challenges scientists face when exploring other planets.

Turn 15.Utterance: Can I try it? Is it hard to control?

Turn 16.Utterance:
Ollie: Absolutely, you can try it! It's located in the Space exhibit on the Mezzanine. 
Ollie: It might be a little tricky at first, but that's part of the fun of learning how astronauts and scientists explore!

Turn 17.Utterance: I think I'll head over there next. Thanks for the tip!

Turn 18.Utterance:
Ollie: You're very welcome! Have an out-of-this-world time exploring! 
Ollie: Come back and tell me all about your Mars mission adventure!

Turn 20.Utterance: Ollie: Bye for now! Happy exploring!

Turn 1.Utterance: Wow, this place is huge! Where should we even start?

Turn 2.Utterance: Hi there! I'm Ollie, your virtual guide from OSU. I'm so glad you're excited! A great place to start is the Energy Explorers exhibit. You can become an Energy Hero and learn how your choices power the world!

Turn 3.Utterance: An Energy Hero? That sounds fun. What kind of choices are we talking about?

Turn 4.Utterance: You get to make choices that save energy, like carpooling with friends or biking to work. You can even find sneaky "energy vampires" in a pretend house!

Turn 5.Utterance: Oh, I tried something like that earlier! I drove a car that was almost out of gas. It was a little stressful trying to make it to the station. Is that the kind of choice you mean?

Turn 6.Utterance: That's exactly it! That driving game is called "Don't Run Out of Gas." It's a fun way to learn about energy use. You can also try to restore power during a pretend blackout!

Turn 7.Utterance: That sounds cool. So, what happens if you find these "energy vampires"?

Turn 8.Utterance: You get to eliminate them! They are things that use power even when you're not using them, like a TV on standby. Finding them helps you save energy at home.

Turn 9.Utterance: That makes sense. I guess I should unplug my phone charger more often. What else is in this area?

Turn 10.Utterance: You're thinking like a true Energy Hero! Nearby, you can see the amazing "Moth Series" sculptures made from recycled materials. They show how old things can become beautiful new art!

Turn 11.Utterance: That's a great idea. I saw some cool sculptures on the way in. Are those part of it?

Turn 12.Utterance: Probably! Artist Michelle Stitzlein created them from recycled objects. They're inspired by real moths and are located on Level 2. It's a wonderful example of creativity and science!

Turn 13.Utterance: They sound really neat. So, what's the connection between the art and the energy stuff? Is it just about recycling?

Turn 14.Utterance: That's a super smart question! Both exhibits are about making thoughtful choices. Energy Explorers is about using resources wisely, and the art shows how we can reuse materials creatively. They're both about caring for our world!

Turn 15.Utterance: That's a really nice way to connect them. It makes the whole place feel more thoughtful. So, what's another exhibit you think we shouldn't miss?

Turn 16.Utterance: You absolutely shouldn't miss the live Rat Basketball show! You can cheer for your favorite rat as they play a thrilling game. It's a live demonstration of science in action!

Turn 17.Utterance: Rat Basketball? Seriously? That sounds... wild. Where do they play?

Turn 18.Utterance: Yes, it's real and so much fun! The show is included with your admission and happens on our Science Onstage. The schedule is available at the Box Office.

Turn 19.Utterance: I have to see that. Is it nearby, or should we head to a different floor?

Turn 20.Utterance: I'm a virtual guide on a screen, so I can't walk with you, but I can tell you the Science Onstage area is easy to find! Check the map or ask a friendly staff member for directions.

Turn 21.Utterance: Perfect, thanks! I'll go find a map. This has been really helpful, Ollie.

Turn 22.Utterance: You're very welcome! Have an amazing time exploring COSI. Remember, science is everywhere, so stay curious! Come back and visit me anytime you have a question.

Turn 23.Utterance: Thanks, Ollie! I'll be sure to come back if I get lost or have another question. See you later!

Turn 24.Utterance: Bye for now! I'll be right here on this screen, ready for your next adventure. Enjoy the rats!

Turn 1.Utterance: That lever to lift the car was amazing! It made me feel so strong. What else around here uses simple machines like that?

Turn 2.Utterance: Ollie: Oh, I'm so glad you tried the lever in Big Science Park! That's a fantastic example of a simple machine. You can find other simple machines all over COSI, like the giant rolling sphere that uses wheels and axles!

Turn 3.Utterance: Are there any inside exhibits about machines or forces?

Turn 4.Utterance: Absolutely! The Energy Explorers exhibit has a fun station where you use a hand crank to power different light bulbs—that's another simple machine at work! [SOURCE: EXHIBIT: Energy

Turn 5.Utterance: Oh, that sounds cool. Where is the Energy Explorers exhibit from here?

Turn 6.Utterance: Ollie: I can tell you it's on the same level as Big Science Park! From where you are, you can head back inside to Level One and look for the signs. It's a whole exhibit about how we power our world.

Turn 7.Utterance: Perfect, thanks! I think I saw some signs for that. While we're on this floor, is there anything else that's really hands-on like the lever?

Turn 8.Utterance: Ollie: You bet! On this level, you could also check out the Life exhibition. It has interactive stations where you can test your strength, flexibility, and even play with cool optical illusions!

Turn 9.Utterance: Oh, testing my strength sounds fun after that lever. Is it close by?

Turn 10.Utterance: Ollie: The Life exhibition is also on Level One, so it should be easy to find from where you are! Just follow the signs—it's a whole adventure about the human body and mind.

Turn 11.Utterance: Great, I'll head that way next. I also really loved the motion simulator earlier. Are there any other exhibits that use technology like that?

Turn 12.Utterance: Ollie: For more awesome technology, you should visit The Science Behind Pixar! It's full of interactive stations where you can try the digital tools that animators use to create movies.

Turn 13.Utterance: That sounds incredible! Is that exhibit included with general admission, or do you need a special ticket?

Turn 14.Utterance: Ollie: The Science Behind Pixar requires a separate ticket in addition to general admission. But it's totally worth it—you get to see how they bring characters like Buzz Lightyear to life!

Turn 15.Utterance: Oh, good to know. I'll have to check with my group about that. Is it on the same floor too?

Turn 16.Utterance: Ollie: I'm not sure which floor that exhibit is on today, but you can find all the current locations on the COSI app or the maps around the museum! Have a fantastic time exploring.

Turn 17.Utterance: Thanks, Ollie! I'll pull up the app and check.

Turn 18.Utterance: Ollie: You're very welcome! I'm so glad I could help. Have a super scientific day exploring everything COSI has to offer!

Turn 20.Utterance: Ollie: Bye for now! Remember, science is everywhere you look!
